_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: LOCAL Cape Canaveral, FL — entire 5-member elected body in one batch (Mayor Morrison + Mayor Pro Tem Kellum + Council Davis + Council Jackson + Council Willis). Two evidence_local catches (Morrison, Willis), three roster_verified (Kellum, Davis, Jackson). NOTABLE: Morrison runs a 3-vote majority bloc with Kellum + Jackson that fired City Manager Todd Morley 3-2 with cause Jan 2025 (governance, not rubric-scored); Davis + Willis are the 2-vote minority that voted to RETAIN Morley. Morrison has 2 hard TRUE (economic_stewardship.q2 largest tax decrease in a decade + refuse_state_overreach.q0 led codified council opposition to FDOT A1A roundabout) plus weak TRUE on refuse_state_overreach.q3 (same A1A action is textbook subsidiarity); 27 NULL; soft deviation context = Cape Canaveral joined Climate Mayors / Paris coalition + glyphosate ban + Morrison's day-job is solar industry (Liberty Watt) — rubric-adjacent but no cell-specific FALSE. Willis has 3 TRUE (public_justice.q0 personally + wife Paula sponsor BCSO Coffee-with-a-Cop + hosted joint BCSO LE-fraud presentation + 'Law and Order' campaign platform meets HIGH BAR; refuse_state_overreach.q0 + q3 from 2025 FLC Home Rule Hero Award for advocacy during 2025 FL Legislative Session against state preemption); BREC-aligned via 2021 YouTube address so party=R set. Kellum (Catholic, Our Saviour Parish; 30+ yr resident; Mickie's Deli owner; VFW Auxiliary), Davis (FL native; retired BCSO deputy; phlebotomist; mother of disabled child; publicly listed by Brevard Democratic Executive Committee so party=D set; biographical LE background is NOT scored TRUE on PJ_q0 per high-bar discipline), Jackson (tech executive turned property manager; ran 2023 on Fiscal Responsibility/Quality of Life) — all roster_verified, 30/30 NULL, no findable theology/culture-cell positions. Cape Canaveral has NOT issued Pride proclamation or rainbow-flag action (no FALSE on bib_marriage.q4), NOT passed 2A sanctuary resolution (no TRUE on self_defense.q1). FL state law preempts municipal sanctuary (SB 168 2019) and FL has permitless carry since 2023, so several rubric cells are state-occupied. Confidence calls follow refine-scorecard SKILL.md: 'roster_verified for confirmed real official with zero findable policy positions; evidence_local only when substantive cited findings exist.' Note that party flips (Davis→D, Willis→R) come from documented public listings/affiliations, not inference from voting — defensible to a hostile reader.

profile.religion: Non-denominational evangelical Christian (LifePointe Ministries, Titusville FL — Vision Team Leader and Men's Group Leader since 2012, per his own LinkedIn)
profile.background: Wesley 'Wes' Morrison (age ~41) is a third-generation Space Coast Floridian whose grandfather managed one of Cape Canaveral's first grocery stores in 1965. Titusville High School graduate; B.A. Political Science (Business minor), Florida State University 2004-2008 (first college graduate in his family). Professionally a Solar Project Developer at Liberty Watt; SEIA member, Top 500 Solar Contractors 2015-2016. Two terms on Cape Canaveral City Council before being elected the city's 2nd-youngest Mayor in November 2021. Re-elected November 5, 2024 with 77.09% (2,477 votes) over Angela Raymond (22.91% / 736 votes); current 3-year term runs 2024-2027. Chairs the city's Community Redevelopment Agency Board. Active Christian church-volunteer life: LifePointe Ministries (non-denominational evangelical Space Coast community church) — Vision Team Leader and Men's Group Leader since 2012; CREW29 homeless / cold-night-shelter ministry involvement. Holds an Environmental Stewardship certificate from the Institute for Humane Studies (Dec 2016). Wife Christina; daughter Parker.

wes-morrison.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: broad-tent nonpartisan Florida coastal-city mayor with a real off-the-dais Christian church-volunteer life (LifePointe Ministries Vision Team Leader since 2012) but ZERO public theology-cell record. Cleanly scored cells = 2 HARD TRUE + 1 weak TRUE: (1) economic_stewardship.q2 — 'passed the City's largest tax decrease in over a decade' per his own campaign site, corroborated by The Local Lens and Space Coast Daily coverage of Council tax-restraint posture; (2) refuse_state_overreach.q0 — led codified 5-0 Council opposition to FDOT's State Road A1A roundabout (Aug 2022) on local-control grounds, quoted: 'they are not going to go against the grain and do something that this community does not want'; (3) refuse_state_overreach.q3 — same A1A action functionally states the subsidiarity principle (city should decide local road design, not FDOT). 27 NULL — small-coastal-city nonpartisan mayor whose public record is roads, lagoon, code enforcement, budget, school closure, and city-manager accountability (led 3-2 Jan 2025 firing of City Manager Todd Morley 'with cause' for 'systemic failures in permitting, infrastructure, and records management'), not life/marriage/gender/election-integrity/2A. SOFT DEVIATION CONTEXT (rubric-adjacent, not a scored FALSE on any cell as currently written): Cape Canaveral joined the Climate Mayors Coalition (Paris-aligned, Clinton-Global-Initiative-seeded) + has 2021 Resiliency Action Plan + explicit 2035 net-zero municipal-building goals + joined the Climate Mayors EV Purchasing Collaborative; Morrison's day-job is solar industry; Council unanimously passed Resolution 2021-03 glyphosate ban while he was a council member. These technocratic / center-left environmental postures cluster but none cite a specific rubric cell's contrary position. Religion = LifePointe Ministries (off-dais Christian volunteer leadership) — does NOT translate to any christian_liberty cell because he has not invoked Christ in public mayoral statements on any rubric topic. Honest NULL on all sanctity/marriage/family/election/border/self-defense cells.

0.note: Searched FB (yesforwes), X (@wesmorrison), IG, campaign site (wesmorrison.com), LinkedIn, Ballotpedia, Florida Today + WFTV + Space Coast Daily 2021-2026 for any abortion/conception/personhood statement; no statement located. City of Cape Canaveral has no abortion jurisdiction.

0.note: No public 1-man-1-woman affirmation located. Marriage to Christina mentioned on campaign site is biographical, not a theological statement.

Cannot score TRUE or FALSE on q0 — honest null.

Goes beyond slogan — specific quantified claim with multi-source corroboration.

Quote: 'Even though they do believe the roundabout is the best solution from their analysis for this community they are not going to go against the grain and do something that this community does not want.' This is concrete asserted local control against a top-down state agency directive.

Cape Canaveral has joined the Climate Mayors Coalition (Paris-aligned) — a posture that leans INTO climate compliance rather than refusing state/federal ESG overreach. Honest null per cell's narrow language ('protected citizens from state overreach') — joining a coalition is not actively unprotecting, but also is not a TRUE; rubric-adjacent deviation context is documented in notes.

3.note: TRUE — the A1A roundabout opposition is textbook subsidiarity in operation: city-level body has determined this is best decided at the local-municipal level, not by FDOT. Same on-record statement as q0.

set.office: Mayor Pro Tem / City Council, City of Cape Canaveral

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (Our Saviour Catholic Parish, Cocoa Beach/Cape Canaveral)
profile.background: Maria 'Mickie' Kellum has lived in Cape Canaveral 30+ years. She owned Mickie's Deli, Inc. at Port Canaveral; she now works part-time for Aging Matters as Site Manager for Seniors At Lunch. Widow of retired tugboat Captain Jeff Kellum (deceased). Mother of two and grandmother of four. Past President of VFW Auxiliary Post 10131 and currently Junior Vice; volunteers cooking breakfast for veterans and residents the third Sunday of every month. Led revitalization of Cape Canaveral's Veterans Park. Volunteer history includes Habitat for Humanity, Little League, and back-to-school backpacks for Cape View Elementary. Elected to Cape Canaveral City Council in 2019 (3-year term); re-elected 2022 and serves as Mayor Pro Tem (term ends Nov 2025; one cityofcapecanaveral.org page already shows Kay Jackson as Mayor Pro Tem so transition may be imminent — official capecanaveral.gov page authoritative as of June 2026 still shows Kellum). Council priorities: A1A traffic safety ('zero traffic-related deaths'), Banana River Lagoon restoration, stormwater/sewer infrastructure, 'keeping taxes, fees and debt low,' and addressing blighted properties.

mickie-kellum.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: Civic-Maintenance Municipal Incumbent — a longtime Catholic (Our Saviour Catholic Parish), VFW-Auxiliary-leading, infrastructure-and-traffic-safety council member whose public record is almost entirely operational and contains ZERO findable footprint on any of the 30 RESOLUTE social/constitutional/theological rubric cells. ROSTER_VERIFIED — Kellum is a confirmed real Cape Canaveral elected official (currently Mayor Pro Tem; term ends Nov 2025), with a verified bio, religion (Catholic), and one substantive governance action on record (joined Morrison + Jackson in Jan 2025 3-2 vote to terminate City Manager Todd Morley 'with cause' for 'restoring accountability, transparency, and trust' and 'systemic failures in permitting, infrastructure, and records management'). However, 30/30 rubric cells are honest NULL because no findable statements or roll-call votes touch the sanctity/marriage/family/liberty/election-integrity/public-justice/sovereignty/border/2A cells. Morley firing is governance, not ideology. 'Keep taxes low' campaign messaging is a vague slogan per rubric discipline (slogans = NULL, not TRUE). No party in nonpartisan office (registration unconfirmed — BREC municipal-candidate roster URL surfaced in search returned 404 on direct fetch).

set.office: City Council, City of Cape Canaveral

profile.background: Kim Davis is a Florida native, 'one of the first babies born at Cape Canaveral Hospital.' Retired Brevard County Sheriff's Office deputy who patrolled Cape Canaveral in the early 1980s; former small-business owner; currently works as a phlebotomist with Health First. Mother of a child with a disability — emphasizes 'cultural inclusion' for persons with disabilities. First elected to Cape Canaveral City Council 2019; re-elected 2022 with ~21% / 2,076 votes (finished 2nd). Publicly listed by the Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee on its official 'Elected Officials' page as a Democrat (party set 'D' on this basis — the office itself is nonpartisan but she chose to be publicly listed by the county Democratic party). Stated council priorities: infrastructure (stormwater/streets/wastewater), environmental sustainability/resiliency, a 'city center' / Civic Hub for multigenerational use, and 'green space' for pollution/heat/flood mitigation. Self-described as 'a good steward of taxpayers' money.' Aug 2025 community FB group post headlined 'Councilmember Kim Davis suffers stroke' surfaced; she is still listed as a sitting member on the official city site as of June 2026 with no subsequent reporting found about resignation or status change. Religion: NOT FOUND in any public source.

kim-davis.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: Locally-focused, low-ideological-footprint Democratic council member — a retired BCSO deputy and small-business-owner-turned-phlebotomist whose entire public record is infrastructure, environmental sustainability/resiliency, disability-inclusion, and a Civic Hub city-center vision, with NO traceable positions on any of the 30 RESOLUTE social/constitutional rubric cells. Partisan signal (publicly listed by Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee = party 'D' set) and policy signal (sustainability/inclusion frame + Civic-Hub spending advocacy + 2-vote minority that VOTED TO RETAIN City Manager Morley against the 3-2 firing majority Jan 2025) both lean center-left, but neither rises to a documented FALSE on any specific cell as currently written. Biographical retired-BCSO-deputy background is NOT scored TRUE on public_justice.q0 per the HIGH BAR discipline (rubric requires affirmative public pro-LE advocacy, not biography). 'Good steward of taxpayers' money' campaign slogan is held at NULL per discipline (vague slogans = NULL). All 30 cells honest NULL — confidence ROSTER_VERIFIED.

set.office: City Council, City of Cape Canaveral

profile.background: Kay Jackson is a Cape Canaveral resident of 19+ years; former Network Engineering Director with Silicon Valley/global tech and project-management background; currently a small-business owner providing property-management services and tech contract work. Serves on the city's Community Redevelopment Agency Board ex officio as a council member. Stated 2023 campaign platform was 'Accountability, Fiscal Responsibility, Community, Quality of Life — For the Citizens!', emphasizing fiscally-responsible budgeting, protecting the Indian River Lagoon, balancing tourism vs. residential quality of life, and prioritizing infrastructure and citizen concerns. Won November 2023 Cape Canaveral City Council seat with 774 votes / 34.93% in a 3-way race vs. incumbent Don Willis and Steven Stroud; 3-year term so her seat is up Nov 2026. Part of the Morrison/Kellum/Jackson 3-vote bloc that in January 2025 voted 3-2 'with cause' to terminate 23-year City Manager Todd Morley, denying him ~$61K severance. Religion: NOT FOUND. Party (registration): NOT FOUND — not on the BREC endorsed-candidate roster, nor on the Brevard Dems elected-officials page; nonpartisan office and personal voter-reg party not surfaced in public search.

kay-jackson.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: Apolitical small-city manager-fiscal type — a former tech executive turned property manager who ran and governs on narrow municipal fiscal/quality-of-life issues (Indian River Lagoon, tourism/residential balance, infrastructure, citizen voice), with NO documented engagement on any of the 30 RESOLUTE Christian-conservative scorecard cells. ROSTER_VERIFIED — confirmed real, sitting Cape Canaveral elected official (2023 election certification, official city bio, January 2025 Morley-termination vote on record), but ZERO findable positions on any of the 30 scorecard cells. Per discipline ('vague slogans = NULL'), 'fiscal responsibility' alone cannot score economic_stewardship.q2 TRUE. The Morley firing is a managerial-accountability action, not an ideological signal scoreable in any rubric cell. All 30 cells honest NULL.

set.office: City Council, City of Cape Canaveral

profile.background: Don Willis is a retired business executive and entrepreneur with 40+ years professional experience; owned Porter Willis (technology consulting) for ~14 years serving international corporations and small businesses. BA Magna Cum Laude, University of North Carolina; Executive Education, UNC Kenan-Flagler Business School; PMP-certified. Before council, served as the city's Parks and Recreation Commissioner, Vice-Chair of the Business and Economic Development Advisory Board (4 years), and on a private school's board of directors. Married to Paula Willis; three grown children, three grandchildren. Elected to Cape Canaveral City Council November 2021 (top vote share ~25.4% of 5,174 votes), re-elected November 2023 with 38.81% (~860 votes). Currently serves as Treasurer of the Space Coast League of Cities; member of the Florida League of Cities Transportation/Intergovernmental Relations/Mobility/Emergency Management Legislative Committee, Cape Canaveral Local Roads Committee, Space Coast TPO Governing Board, FDOT District 5 Central Florida Safety Strategic Plan Steering Committee, and FLC Federal Action Strike Team. Recipient of the 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award for advocacy during the 2025 Legislative Session. Personally aligned with the Brevard Republican Executive Committee (party 'R' set on basis of 2021 BREC YouTube address). Religion: NOT FOUND (served on board of an unnamed private school; denomination not specified; the LinkedIn 'Don Willis - Pastor at CrossBridge Baptist' result is a DIFFERENT person, NOT this Don Willis). Part of the Davis-Willis 2-vote minority that voted to RETAIN City Manager Todd Morley in the 3-2 January 2025 termination vote.

don-willis.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: Republican-aligned, business-executive, 'Home Rule + Law-and-Order' local-government technocrat — affirmatively pro-police (cleared HIGH BAR via personal Coffee-with-a-Cop sponsorship + joint BCSO LE-fraud presentation + 'Law and Order' campaign platform) and demonstrably anti-state-preemption (2025 FLC Home Rule Hero), with a near-total NULL footprint on the cultural cells. Three HARD TRUE: (1) public_justice.q0 — he and wife Paula are NAMED sponsors of BCSO 'Coffee with a Cop' (per his own city bio: 'sponsoring Coffee with a Cop'), affirmatively hosted joint Council Member + BCSO LE-fraud public-safety presentation on Identity Theft/Fraud/Scams, and campaign explicitly platformed on 'Law and Order, keeping citizens safe from crime on our streets' — pattern of affirmative engagement beyond routine budget votes meets HIGH BAR; (2) refuse_state_overreach.q0 — 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award recipient explicitly recognized for advocacy DURING the 2025 Florida Legislative Session against state preemption of local authority; (3) refuse_state_overreach.q3 — same Home Rule Hero designation + active service on FLC Legislative Policy Committee and Federal Action Strike Team is by definition subsidiarity advocacy (lowest-competent-level decision-making vs. state/federal preemption). 27 NULL — quiet civility-coded transportation-and-budget-focused councilmember whose only RESOLUTE-visible profile is PJ.q0 + refuse_q0 + refuse_q3. No FALSE-cluster — zero documented deviations against the rubric on culture-war cells, but silence is not affirmation in a small ~10K coastal city where most council business is stormwater, code enforcement, and CRA millage rather than life/marriage/gender. Faction signal: voted to RETAIN City Manager Morley in the Jan 2025 3-2 firing vote (institutional/establishment side of the controversy rather than reform/firing-faction).
